description: Build an AI-driven browser automation platform with multi-engine support and a unified interface.

userStories.title: Unified Automation Interface
userStories.description: Define a core architecture and unified TypeScript interface that abstracts different browser automation engines (Browser-use, Stagehand, etc.).
acceptanceCriteria: Create 'AutomationEngine' interface with methods like navigate(), click(), type(), and screenshot()
Implement a factory or registry to load specific engine wrappers
Define common error types for automation failures
Unit test: interface methods are correctly defined and typed
userStories.technicalNotes: Use TypeScript interfaces. Ensure abstraction is generic enough for both DOM-based and vision-based engines.

userStories.title: Browser-use Engine Wrapper
userStories.description: Implement a wrapper for the Browser-use Python library, allowing it to be controlled through the unified interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Bridge TypeScript calls to Python 'browser-use' (via shell/IPC or a lightweight API)
Handle session state and screenshot capture from Browser-use
Verify basic navigation and click operations through the wrapper
userStories.technicalNotes: Browser-use is Python-based; implemented via a FastAPI bridge server managed by a PythonBridgeEngine base class.

userStories.title: Stagehand Engine Wrapper
userStories.description: Implement a wrapper for the Stagehand (Playwright-based) framework, integrating it into the unified interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize Stagehand/Playwright context and handle page lifecycle
Implement core actions (click, type, navigate) using Stagehand's high-level API
Verify reliable selector-based or AI-based actions via Stagehand
userStories.technicalNotes: Stagehand is Node.js-native, integrated directly using its Playwright-based API.

userStories.title: Natural Language CLI Entry Point
userStories.description: Create a basic CLI that accepts natural language commands and routes them to the appropriate engine.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI tool 'ai-vision' that takes a text prompt as input
Basic routing logic to select an engine (or default to Browser-use)
Log execution steps to the console and to a local SQLite database
Basic session summary output after task completion
userStories.technicalNotes: Uses 'commander' and 'node:sqlite' (built-in Node 24 feature) for history tracking.

userStories.title: Remove Stagehand engine
acceptance: src/engines/stagehand/ directory deleted
StagehandEngine removed from registry and exports
No stagehand references remain in src/
npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm test passes
npm run build passes

userStories.title: YAML workflow loader
acceptance: workflows/*.yaml directory exists with at least post_to_reddit.yaml
src/orchestrator/loader.ts loads and validates YAML against zod schema
CLI workflow command accepts a .yaml file path in addition to built-in names
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Markdown instruction loader
acceptance: instructions/orchestrator.md exists
instructions/author.md exists
instructions/executor.md exists
src/orchestrator/loader.ts reads and returns instruction markdown
Content is injected into Claude context at session start

userStories.title: New MCP tools: write_copy, query_telemetry, read_memory, write_memory
acceptance: mcp/server.ts exposes write_copy tool calling GeminiWriter
mcp/server.ts exposes query_telemetry tool reading telemetry DB
mcp/server.ts exposes read_memory and write_memory tools
mcp/server.ts exposes list_workflows tool listing workflows/*.yaml
All tools have zod-validated input schemas
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Claude orchestrator loop
acceptance: src/orchestrator/loop.ts implements Claude tool-calling agent loop
Loop reads YAML workflow + md instructions before first tool call
Loop reads memory bank context before first tool call
Loop enforces permissions.require_human_approval_before from YAML
workflowEngine.run() delegates to orchestrator loop when YAML source
End-to-end: workflow write_and_post_to_reddit completes via Claude loop
npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: Memory bank seeding
acceptance: memory/bank/ directory created
memory/bank/world.md seeded with known platform quirks from session history
memory/bank/platform/reddit.md seeded with r/test and r/artificial observations
memory/bank/user_preferences.md created with defaults
bank-reader.ts reads and formats bank files for Claude context injection

userStories.title: browser-use live event bridge
userStories.description: Reconciled 2026-05-01: complete with evidence. The browser-use bridge now posts action events to `BROWSER_USE_CALLBACK_URL`, the Node bridge emits telemetry, the UI forwards live browser-use events, the orchestrator consumes them without polling, and the repo now has passing typecheck/test validation.
acceptance: browser-use Python server POSTs action events to BROWSER_USE_CALLBACK_URL
Node.js python-bridge receives and emits telemetry for each browser-use action
UI WebSocket forwards browser_use.action.* events to client in real-time
Orchestrator loop receives mid-task browser state without polling
npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: Webhooks — inbound triggers and outbound notifications
acceptance: src/webhooks/server.ts exposes POST /webhooks/trigger accepting {workflow_id, params}
Inbound request validates signature (WEBHOOK_SECRET) before executing workflow
Workflow result POSTed to configured callback_url if provided in trigger payload
Outbound POST includes {workflow_id, success, outputs, durationMs, timestamp}
webhook_secret stored via vault:export, never in plaintext env
src/webhooks/server.ts registers with Express/Fastify app on AI_VISION_WEBHOOK_PORT
npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: TSC crash diagnostic classification baseline
userStories.description: Reconciled 2026-05-01: archived-complete. The repo contains the diagnostic bug report and YAML story artifact, and later Node 24 parity evidence plus EN-006 remediation work absorbed the active follow-through. This story should remain archived as the baseline diagnostic record rather than stay open as active backlog.
acceptance: Investigation flow records local Node, pnpm, TypeScript, and `NODE_OPTIONS` before reproducing the crash
Investigation compares local behavior against the CI-pinned Node 24 baseline before blaming the TypeScript graph
Investigation captures V8 abort evidence, file-count shape, and extended diagnostics values including `Instantiations`, `Memory used`, and `Check time`
Investigation captures Linux OOM evidence from `dmesg`, `free -h`, `ulimit -a`, and cgroup memory files
Final report classifies the failure as OS/kernel, Node/V8 runtime, or TypeScript checker path with evidence for the ruled-in and ruled-out layers

Story is complete only when a bug report artifact and YAML refactoring artifact both exist, record the observed crash signature, runtime baseline, Node 24 comparison result, layer-by-layer evidence, graphed metrics, and clear refactoring procedures for the ruled-in bug path
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when `docs/artifacts/tsc-crash-forge-story.yaml` authors the story with layers, shapes, graphed data, and bug-refactoring procedures, and the paired bug report artifact records a final ruled-in or ruled-out classification across OS/kernel, Node/V8, and TypeScript checker layers.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the investigation yields an explicit error or bug report with concise refactoring procedures, names the ruled-in layer, rules out the competing layers with evidence, and persists the result in the FORGE YAML story artifact plus a human-readable report.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from `docs/debriefs/tsc_crash_error_investigation.md`. Preserve the `SIGABRT` versus `SIGKILL` distinction, treat Node 24 parity as the first discriminator, and use `docs/artifacts/tsc-crash-forge-story.yaml` as the structured story-authoring artifact. Reconciled on 2026-05-01 as archived-complete historical evidence, not active backlog.

userStories.title: TSC crash layered remediation protocol
userStories.description: Reconciled 2026-05-01: retired as superseded by the completed EN-006 remediation evidence and the now-clean typecheck path. The originally planned standalone US-021 remediation artifact set was never completed under this story id, so this entry should not remain open as active implementation backlog.
acceptance: Remediation protocol records local Node, pnpm, TypeScript, and `NODE_OPTIONS` and treats CI-pinned Node 24 parity as the first remediation gate
Remediation protocol separates `SIGABRT` Node/V8 evidence from `SIGKILL` or kernel/cgroup evidence and routes them to different remediation paths
TypeScript refactoring is blocked until Node 24 parity and OS kill-path checks are ruled out with evidence
Remediation result persists as a classified bug report that names the ruled-in layer, rules out competing layers, and provides concise refactoring procedures

Story is complete only when a YAML remediation story artifact and a paired bug report remediation artifact both exist and record layers, shapes, graphed metrics, and remediation procedures for the ruled-in path
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when `docs/artifacts/tsc-crash-remediation-forge-story.yaml` and its paired remediation bug report artifact both exist, runtime parity has been checked or explicitly documented as unavailable, and the final report names the ruled-in remediation layer with concise procedures and ruled-out competing layers.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the remediation workflow produces an explicit error or bug report that identifies whether the active remediation path is OS/kernel, Node/V8 runtime, or TypeScript checker, and persists the required refactoring procedures in both a human-readable report and a FORGE YAML story artifact.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from the consolidated remediation requirements in `docs/debriefs/ts-memory-troubleshooting.md`, `docs/debriefs/tsc_crash_error_investigation.md`, `docs/debriefs/tsc-crash-bug-report.md`, and `docs/artifacts/tsc-crash-forge-story.yaml`. Reconciled on 2026-05-01 as superseded by completed EN-006 tracker evidence plus current passing typecheck evidence; preserve the layered order as historical context only.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer design
userStories.description: Design the direct workflow gate layer that makes the TypeScript engine the authoritative workflow kernel for skip logic, approval enforcement, HITL publication, side-effect policy, and output validation, without deleting `mode: agentic` yet.
acceptance: Produce a gate-layer design artifact that defines `GateDecision`, `GateContext`, and the trace/state surfaces required by the direct engine
Map the exact gate insertion points in `src/workflow/engine.ts` for before-step, in-step, post-step, HITL, side-effect, and wrap-up boundaries
Define the canonical HITL state publication model across `hitlCoordinator`, `workflowEngine.currentState`, `/api/status`, and websocket projection
Define how approval and side-effect policies block Python/browser-use execution before irreversible browser mutations
Define the direct-path validation rules for empty, placeholder, unresolved, and context-invalid generated outputs
Define the minimum test matrix required before `mode: agentic` can be quarantined or retired
Do not implement the gate layer yet and do not remove `mode: agentic` in this story
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when the repo contains a Forge storyline, YAML story card, implementation handoff, and explicit definition-of-done for the direct workflow gate layer design, and the design package clearly identifies the future gate interfaces, insertion points, state owners, and test map without changing runtime behavior.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the direct workflow gate layer has a governed design package that is specific enough for a later implementation story to execute without re-discovering the architecture, and the package explicitly keeps TypeScript as the workflow kernel, Python as the bounded intelligence layer, Rust out of scope, and `mode: agentic` still present until direct-path gates are proven.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from `docs/architecture/as-built_execution_atlas.md` and `docs/debriefs/Discovery_mode-agentic.md`. Keep the story design-only: interface and placement first, implementation later.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer implementation - Phase 1 HITL state publication
userStories.description: Implement the first direct-path gate-layer slice by making the TypeScript workflow engine publish every direct HITL wait and terminal transition through one canonical public state path, without removing `mode: agentic` or widening into approval and side-effect enforcement yet.
acceptance: Implement a canonical state publication helper in `src/workflow/engine.ts` for direct-path running, wait, complete, and error transitions
Every direct HITL wait publishes a visible operator state (`phase` plus `hitlAction`) before the blocking wait begins
`workflowEngine.currentState` remains the canonical public workflow projection while `hitlCoordinator` remains the blocking wait owner
`/api/status` and websocket/UI projection reflect the same converged direct-engine state for every direct wait and terminal transition
Regression tests cover `awaiting_human`, `pii_wait`, `hitl_qa`, final confirmation, and terminal `complete` / `error` visibility
`mode: agentic` remains present and untouched in this story
`pnpm run typecheck` passes
`pnpm test` passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when the direct engine publishes all direct HITL waits and terminal transitions through one canonical visible state path, the corresponding regression tests pass, and the story leaves approval, side-effect, and skip-gate implementation for later work.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the direct workflow engine has a working Phase 1 gate-layer implementation for canonical HITL/public-state publication, operators can see every human-action state reliably through the existing UI/status surfaces, and the repo remains on the current strategic path: TypeScript kernel, Python bounded worker, `mode: agentic` not yet retired.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from `docs/artifacts/2026-04-24-direct-workflow-gate-layer-design.md`, especially the state ownership and `publishStateTransition()` sections. Keep this story narrow: implement state publication only, not approval or side-effect enforcement.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer — Phase 2: Final-step confirmation pre-flight binding enforcement
userStories.description: Enforce a strict run-binding pre-flight check on /api/confirm-final-step so stale-tab and session-mismatched confirmation requests are rejected before reaching hitlCoordinator. Add equivalent caller-attribution trace instrumentation to /api/return-control. Implements the enforcement layer documented in docs/debriefs/2026-04-25-confirm-final-step-trace-and-preflight-requirements.md based on the attribution matrix evidence in downloads/live-repro-matrix-evidence.json.
acceptance: Run-phase gate rejects HTTP 409 when workflowEngine.currentState.phase !== 'hitl_qa'
Session binding gate rejects HTTP 400 when requestSessionId is present and mismatches the active session ID
WebSocket presence gate rejects HTTP 403 when resolvedClientId is non-empty and socketsForPage(resolvedClientId).size === 0
Each rejected gate emits ui.hitl.confirm_final_step.rejected with gate name, reason, requestSessionId, activeSessionId, resolvedClientId, matchingWsClientCount, and full caller attribution
Active-tab happy path (all gates pass) succeeds end-to-end and reaches hitlCoordinator unchanged
/api/return-control emits ui.hitl.return_control.received with caller attribution fields (trace only, no enforcement)
Regression tests cover all three rejection paths and the active-tab happy path
pnpm run typecheck passes
pnpm test passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when all three pre-flight gates are implemented and tested on /api/confirm-final-step, /api/return-control has trace attribution parity, and all regression tests pass under typecheck and test gates.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means stale-tab and session-mismatched final-step confirmation requests are rejected before reaching hitlCoordinator, every rejection emits a structured telemetry event, /api/return-control traces caller attribution at parity with /api/confirm-final-step, and all regression tests pass.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/artifacts/2026-04-26-us024-rf006-confirmation-preflight-storyline.md and docs/debriefs/2026-04-25-confirm-final-step-trace-and-preflight-requirements.md. The instrumentation (callerMetadata, socketsForPage, resolvedClientId, wsConnectionCount) is already in src/ui/server.ts from the prior investigation pass — this story adds the enforcement gates on top. Rejection telemetry must be emitted through one handler-scoped named const emitConfirmationRejection placed immediately after the existing /api/confirm-final-step attribution values are computed. Non-UI callers with empty resolvedClientId are not blocked in this story (deferred to a caller-allowlist design story).

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer — Phase 3: Return-control pre-flight binding enforcement
userStories.description: Enforce active-run and active-client pre-flight binding on POST /api/return-control so invalid phase/action, session-mismatched, and stale-tab resume requests are rejected before reaching hitlCoordinator.returnControl().
acceptance: Browser UI returnControl() sends sessionId, requestId, clientId, Content-Type application/json, and X-AiVision-Client-Id
Return-control action gate rejects HTTP 409 unless active state is one of: awaiting_human:return_control, awaiting_human:verify_authentication, hitl_qa:approve_draft, hitl_qa:capture_notes
Session binding gate rejects HTTP 400 when requestSessionId is present and mismatches the active session ID
WebSocket presence gate rejects HTTP 403 when resolvedClientId is non-empty and socketsForPage(resolvedClientId) is empty
Each rejected gate emits ui.hitl.return_control.rejected with gate name, reason, request/session/client/socket fields, current phase/action/step, and caller attribution
Active-tab happy paths for all four allowed wait pairs reach hitlCoordinator.returnControl()
Successful resume emits ui.hitl.return_control.completed with attribution fields matching ui.hitl.return_control.received
Regression tests cover all three rejection paths, all four allowed wait-pair happy paths, rejection telemetry, completed telemetry, and UI request binding
pnpm run typecheck passes
pnpm test passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when all three return-control pre-flight gates are implemented and tested on /api/return-control, the browser UI sends run/client binding fields, rejection and completed telemetry are emitted, and all regression tests pass under typecheck and test gates.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means return-control cannot resume a HITL wait from an invalid phase/action, mismatched session, and stale page client, while valid active-tab resume actions still work and all tests pass.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/artifacts/2026-04-26-us025-rf007-return-control-binding-storyline.md and docs/debriefs/2026-04-26-hitl-gate-story-reference.md. US-024 added return-control attribution telemetry only; this story adds enforcement before hitlCoordinator.returnControl(). Rejection telemetry must be emitted through one handler-scoped named const emitReturnControlRejection placed immediately after request-local attribution values are computed. Empty-client non-UI callers are not blocked in this story.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer — Phase 4: Approval gate before browser side effects
userStories.description: Implement direct-engine approval gating before protected browser side-effect steps so permissions.require_human_approval_before is enforced in the direct workflow kernel before executeStep reaches Python/browser-use side effects.
acceptance: Direct workflow engine initializes run-scoped approval state for each workflow run
Direct path honors permissions.require_human_approval_before for configured step ids and step types before executeStep side effects
Approval gate publishes visible hitl_qa state with hitlAction approve_step before blocking
US-025 return-control action gate allows hitl_qa:approve_step so approved side-effect gates can resume through the existing bound endpoint
Approved step id and step type are recorded in run-scoped approval state before side-effect execution
Protected side-effect step cannot execute before approval is recorded
Approval state is consumed after the protected step completes and does not leak across runs
Approval gate emits structured telemetry for required, waiting, approved, and consumed decisions with workflow/session/step context
Regression tests prove submission, agent_task, and browser side-effect steps do not execute before approval
mode: agentic remains present and untouched
pnpm run typecheck passes
pnpm test passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when direct workflows enforce approval before protected side-effect execution, hitl_qa:approve_step resumes through the existing bound return-control endpoint, approval state is run-scoped and consumed, and all regression tests pass under typecheck and test gates.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means a direct workflow cannot execute a protected browser side-effect step until the active UI approval wait has been published, bound to the current run/client context, resumed, and recorded in the run-scoped approval ledger.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/artifacts/2026-04-27-us026-rf008-approval-side-effect-gate-storyline.md, docs/artifacts/2026-04-24-direct-workflow-gate-layer-design.md, and docs/architecture/as-built_execution_atlas.md. Use the direct run loop before executeStep as the gate insertion point. Use existing /api/return-control binding by extending the allowed wait pairs with hitl_qa:approve_step. Do not add a second approval endpoint. Do not remove mode: agentic.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer — Phase 5: Content/output validation gate
userStories.description: Implement direct-path content/output validation so invalid generated body/title values and unresolved downstream placeholders fail before browser posting steps execute.
acceptance: Direct workflows validate generate_content outputKey immediately after writer output is stored
Direct workflows validate outputTitleKey when a generated title is required
Direct workflows validate preflight-provided output before skipping generate_content
Empty, whitespace-only, placeholder, TODO, TBD, Lorem ipsum, and bracketed generated placeholder values fail fast
Downstream unresolved placeholders fail before browser side-effect steps reach executeStep
Invalid output prevents agent_task, fill, type, click, and navigate side effects
Validation telemetry emits workflow.output_validation.passed and workflow.output_validation.failed with workflow/session/step/output context
Valid ai-vision body and title content pass validation
RF-001 same-run substitution behavior still passes
mode: agentic remains present and untouched
pnpm run typecheck passes
pnpm test passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when invalid generated output and unresolved downstream placeholders cannot reach browser side-effect steps, validation telemetry records pass/fail decisions, and regression tests pass under typecheck and test gates.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means direct workflows reject invalid generated body/title values, invalid preflight output, and unresolved downstream placeholders before browser posting steps execute, while valid ai-vision content and existing substitution behavior still pass.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/artifacts/2026-04-27-us027-rf009-content-output-validation-storyline.md, docs/artifacts/2026-04-24-direct-workflow-gate-layer-design.md, docs/architecture/as-built_execution_atlas.md, and docs/debriefs/2026-04-26-hitl-gate-story-reference.md. Keep this story scoped to content/output validation. Do not implement Reddit duplicate evidence scoring, browser postconditions, generalized skip gates, agent_task side-effect policy, or mode: agentic removal.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer — Phase 6: Reddit duplicate-check deterministic evidence gate
userStories.description: Implement a Reddit-specific duplicate-check evidence gate so Reddit submit steps require extracted visible titles, overlap scores, and a deterministic duplicate decision before browser submission.
acceptance: Reddit duplicate-check prompts request a structured evidence contract with result, extracted titles, overlap scores, and matching title when duplicate risk exists
Direct engine parses duplicate-check output into deterministic evidence
Parsed evidence is stored in workflow outputs
submit_reddit_post fails before execution when duplicate evidence is missing
submit_reddit_post fails before execution when extracted titles are missing
submit_reddit_post fails before execution when overlap scores are missing or invalid
submit_reddit_post fails before execution when DUPLICATE_RISK is reported
submit_reddit_post fails before execution when any score is >= 0.70 but the result says NO_DUPLICATE_FOUND
Valid NO_DUPLICATE_FOUND evidence allows submit path to continue
Duplicate-check telemetry records parsed, failed, and duplicate-risk decisions
mode: agentic remains present and untouched
pnpm run typecheck passes
pnpm test passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when Reddit submit steps require deterministic duplicate-check evidence with extracted titles and overlap scores, invalid or duplicate-risk evidence blocks submit, valid evidence allows submit, and all regression tests pass under typecheck and test gates.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means Reddit posting workflows cannot execute submit_reddit_post unless duplicate-check evidence from the same run contains extracted visible titles, valid overlap scores, and a no-duplicate decision below the 0.70 threshold.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/artifacts/2026-04-27-us028-rf010-reddit-duplicate-evidence-storyline.md, docs/debriefs/2026-04-26-hitl-gate-story-reference.md, workflows/post_to_reddit.yaml, and workflows/write_and_post_to_reddit.yaml. Keep this story Reddit-specific. Do not implement generic browser postconditions, generalized skip gates, agent_task side-effect policy, Reddit cleanup automation, or mode: agentic removal.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer — Phase 7: Browser side-effect/postcondition gate
userStories.description: Implement direct-path browser postcondition validation after side-effect steps so prompt-dependent browser success cannot advance the workflow without deterministic URL and output evidence.
acceptance: Side-effect steps can declare expectedUrlAfter, requiredOutputIncludes, and postconditionRequired metadata
Direct engine validates postconditions after executeStep and before downstream steps continue
submit_reddit_post requires current URL to contain /comments/
submit_reddit_post requires output evidence containing a Reddit comments URL
Reddit draft steps can enforce expected subreddit submit URL
Required postcondition failure stops the workflow before downstream confirmation
Postcondition pass telemetry emits workflow.browser_postcondition.passed
Postcondition failure telemetry emits workflow.browser_postcondition.failed
Final HITL confirmation remains present and unchanged
mode: agentic remains present and untouched
pnpm run typecheck passes
pnpm test passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when side-effect steps can declare and enforce deterministic browser postconditions, Reddit submit cannot falsely succeed without comments URL evidence, telemetry records pass/fail decisions, and all regression tests pass under typecheck and test gates.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means direct workflows validate browser side-effect outcomes after execution, block false success before downstream steps, and keep final HITL confirmation as the human-visible verification layer.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/artifacts/2026-04-27-us029-rf011-browser-postcondition-gate-storyline.md, docs/artifacts/2026-04-24-direct-workflow-gate-layer-design.md, docs/architecture/as-built_execution_atlas.md, and docs/debriefs/2026-04-26-hitl-gate-story-reference.md. Keep this story scoped to post-side-effect evidence. Do not implement generalized skip gates, full agent_task side-effect policy, Python/browser-use internals, final HITL replacement, or mode: agentic removal.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer — Phase 8: Generalized precondition/skip gate
userStories.description: Implement a generalized direct-path precondition gate before executeStep so the engine can deterministically run, skip, fail, and request HITL before side effects begin.
acceptance: Direct engine has one precondition gate before executeStep and before approval gating
Authenticated human_takeover with satisfied authVerification skips before HITL wait publication
Unauthenticated human_takeover with unsatisfied authVerification still publishes the HITL wait
generate_content skips writer invocation when valid outputKey already exists
generate_content fails before skip when preflight output exists but fails validation
navigate skips sessionManager.navigate when current URL already matches the resolved target URL
navigate runs normally when current URL does not match the resolved target URL
Unresolved downstream placeholders still fail before browser side effects
Skipped steps are recorded as successful traceable step results
Precondition telemetry emits evaluated, skipped, and failed decisions with workflow/session/step context
Approval, content validation, duplicate evidence, and browser postcondition gates still pass
mode: agentic remains present and untouched
pnpm run typecheck passes
pnpm test passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when the direct run loop has one deterministic precondition gate that traces run, skip, fail, and HITL decisions before executeStep, existing skip behavior is lifted into that gate, redundant navigation is skipped, and all regression tests pass under typecheck and test gates.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means direct workflows make deterministic pre-execution decisions through one traceable precondition gate, skip already-satisfied steps safely, fail unsafe preconditions before side effects, and preserve the downstream gate stack.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/artifacts/2026-04-27-us030-rf012-precondition-skip-gate-storyline.md, docs/artifacts/2026-04-24-direct-workflow-gate-layer-design.md, docs/architecture/as-built_execution_atlas.md, and docs/debriefs/2026-04-26-hitl-gate-story-reference.md. Keep this story scoped to pre-execution decisions. Do not implement full agent_task side-effect policy, Python/browser-use internals, final HITL replacement, or mode: agentic removal.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer — Phase 9: agent_task side-effect safety gate
userStories.description: Implement an agent_task side-effect safety boundary so prompt-driven worker dispatch cannot bypass approval, content, duplicate-evidence, browser postcondition, and precondition gates.
acceptance: Direct engine classifies agent_task prompt side-effect intent before worker dispatch
Protected agent_task side effects cannot dispatch without required approval evidence
Posting-style agent_task side effects cannot dispatch without valid content evidence
Reddit submit-style agent_task side effects cannot dispatch without valid duplicate-check evidence
Read-only agent_task prompts still run normally
Browser postcondition validation still applies after protected agent_task execution
Side-effect safety telemetry emits evaluated, allowed, and blocked decisions with workflow/session/step context
Existing precondition, approval, content validation, duplicate evidence, and browser postcondition gates remain intact
Final HITL confirmation remains present and unchanged
mode: agentic remains present and untouched
pnpm run typecheck passes
pnpm test passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when agent_task prompt-driven side effects are classified before worker dispatch, protected actions cannot bypass direct-path gates, telemetry records allowed and blocked decisions, and all regression tests pass under typecheck and test gates.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means prompt-driven agent_task execution cannot submit, publish, fill, login, perform final clicks, or mutate external browser state unless the direct workflow gate stack has authorized and validated the action.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/artifacts/2026-04-28-us031-rf013-agent-task-side-effect-safety-storyline.md, docs/artifacts/2026-04-24-direct-workflow-gate-layer-design.md, docs/architecture/as-built_execution_atlas.md, and docs/debriefs/2026-04-26-hitl-gate-story-reference.md. Keep this story scoped to agent_task dispatch safety. Do not remove agent_task, modify Python/browser-use internals, replace final HITL confirmation, or remove mode: agentic.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer — Phase 10: agent_task dominant intent classification fix
userStories.description: Fix agent_task intent classification so dominant protected workflow intent wins over lower-level fallback action text while preserving conservative safety gates.
acceptance: Classifier collects all matched intent signals before selecting final intent
Exact live workflows/post_to_reddit.yaml submit_reddit_post prompt classifies as submit
Submit plus fallback fill prompt classifies as submit
Standalone fill prompt still classifies as fill
Standalone login prompt still classifies as login
Duplicate-check read-only prompt classifies as read_only
Reddit submit-style prompt still requires duplicate evidence
Protected submit still receives browser postcondition validation after dispatch
Telemetry includes selected intent and matched intent signals
post_to_reddit.yaml is not patched as a workaround
mode: agentic remains present and untouched
pnpm run typecheck passes
pnpm test passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when the production Reddit submit prompt classifies as dominant submit intent, fallback fill wording no longer causes a fill-intent block, US-031 safety gates remain conservative, and all regression tests pass under typecheck and test gates.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means direct workflows can classify mixed-intent agent_task prompts by dominant workflow intent, the live Reddit submit prompt is covered by regression tests, and no workflow patch is needed to bypass the classifier drift.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/debriefs/2026-04-28-us031-agent-task-dominant-intent-drift.md. Keep this story scoped to classifier semantics and tests. Do not patch temporary workflows, modify Python/browser-use internals, remove agent_task, or remove mode: agentic.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer — Phase 11: live workflow prompt contract regression suite
userStories.description: Add exact live workflow prompt contract coverage and a deterministic safety exception for evidence-producing read-only duplicate-check steps so Reddit duplicate checks can create evidence before submit gating requires it.
acceptance: Tests load exact live agent_task prompts from workflows/post_to_reddit.yaml
Exact live check_duplicate_reddit_post prompt can run before duplicate evidence exists
Exact live check_duplicate_reddit_post prompt records evidence-producing read-only classifier details
Exact live submit_reddit_post prompt remains selected as submit
submit_reddit_post without duplicate evidence remains blocked
submit_reddit_post with valid no-duplicate evidence can reach worker dispatch
Duplicate-risk evidence still blocks submit
Duplicate-check output parsing still stores reddit_duplicate_check_evidence, reddit_duplicate_check_result, and reddit_duplicate_matching_title
Classifier telemetry includes selected intent and matched signals for live prompts
Existing US-028 through US-032 tests still pass
mode: agentic remains present and untouched
pnpm run typecheck passes
pnpm test passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when exact live workflow prompts are contract-tested, duplicate-check evidence production is not circularly blocked, submit remains protected by valid duplicate evidence, and all regression tests pass under typecheck and test gates.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means direct workflows can run evidence-producing read-only duplicate checks without pre-existing duplicate evidence while still blocking Reddit submit until valid no-duplicate evidence exists.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/debriefs/2026-04-28-us031-agent-task-dominant-intent-drift.md. Keep this story scoped to live prompt contract coverage and the duplicate-check evidence-producer seam. Do not patch workflow YAML as a workaround, weaken submit evidence checks, modify Python/browser-use internals, remove agent_task, or remove mode: agentic.

userStories.title: Backlog reconciliation: US-012, US-020, US-021
userStories.description: Reconcile stale backlog state for US-012, US-020, and US-021 against current repo evidence, decide final close/retire status, and align PRD, tracker, progress, and FORGE history naming so future agents do not reopen stale work.
acceptance: US-012 is closed with evidence or left open with one explicit unmet acceptance item
US-020 is explicitly classified as archived-complete or retired
US-021 is explicitly classified as completed or retired as superseded
Current source, tests, progress, and history evidence are cited for each decision
PRD, tracker, progress, and FORGE history naming align for US-012, US-020, and US-021
No separate implementation stories are created for this backlog cleanup
jq empty prd.json passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when US-012, US-020, and US-021 have one explicit evidence-backed final backlog status each, and the naming/status drift across PRD, tracker, progress, and FORGE history has been resolved.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the repository has one governed reconciliation result for US-012, US-020, and US-021, including aligned story names and statuses across PRD, tracker, progress, and FORGE history, so stale work is not reopened by story-id drift.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/debriefs/2026-04-26-hitl-gate-story-reference.md, docs/debriefs/tsc-crash-bug-report.md, and current FORGE history/tracker drift. Keep this story scoped to backlog reconciliation and naming/status alignment; do not split it into three implementation stories.

userStories.title: Screenshot security policy design
userStories.description: Design the screenshot security policy layer so screenshot pixels are classified, retained, blocked, audited, or persisted consistently across live UI, MCP, workflow, orchestrator, browser-use, filesystem, SQLite, and wrap-up boundaries.
acceptance: Design defines screenshot classes including live_frame, debug_frame, evidence, and sensitive_blocked
Design defines sensitivity states and capture behavior during pii_wait, sensitive-target steps, and private account/page contexts
Design defines retention modes for live, debug, rolling, and evidence screenshots
Design defines durable persistence rules for filesystem images, workflow_runs.result_json, wrap-up artifacts, and session_screenshots
Design defines whether screenshot base64 may be persisted and how old result JSON with base64 is handled
Design defines whether evidence screenshots require encryption at rest
Design defines whether GET /api/screenshot requires active client/session binding
Design defines MCP screenshot access and audit expectations
Design splits follow-on implementation into payload contract, persistence sanitization, sensitive gate, and cleanup stories
No runtime screenshot behavior is changed in this design-only story
jq empty prd.json passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when the repo contains a governed screenshot security policy design that covers every screenshot-producing branch and leaves follow-on implementation stories with clear scope boundaries.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means screenshots are no longer treated as undefined neutral artifacts: the design defines classes, sensitivity, retention, persistence, access/audit policy, legacy base64 handling, and implementation sequencing before any runtime screenshot refactor begins.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/debriefs/2026-04-26-screenshot-architecture-shape-layers-modules.md, docs/debriefs/2026-05-01-screenshot-architecture-findings-investigation.md, and docs/debriefs/2026-05-01-screenshot-workflow-investigation-scratch-pad.md. Keep this story design-only.

userStories.title: Screenshot Payload Contract
userStories.description: Implement the first runtime screenshot security-policy slice by introducing a canonical screenshot payload/container, making UI rendering MIME-aware, rendering browser-use action screenshots when present, and replacing raw orchestrator screenshot base64 outputs with payload containers.
acceptance: ScreenshotPayload contract exists with source, class, mimeType, sensitivity, retention, and persistBase64 metadata
Live UI screenshot websocket payloads include ScreenshotPayload while preserving screenshotBase64 compatibility
UI screenshot rendering uses payload MIME type instead of hardcoded JPEG
UI renders browser-use action screenshots live when screenshotBase64 is present
Browser-use action events infer image/jpeg or image/png from base64 magic where possible
Orchestrator screenshot tool stores a JSON ScreenshotPayload container under output_key instead of raw base64
Persistence sanitization, sensitive-phase gates, and rolling cleanup remain out of scope
jq empty prd.json passes
pnpm run typecheck passes
Focused tests pass for python bridge, orchestrator loop, and UI server screenshot rendering contract
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when screenshot-bearing live/runtime boundaries share a payload container shape, browser-use action screenshots can render live with MIME-aware UI handling, orchestrator screenshots no longer write raw base64 outputs, and focused tests plus typecheck pass.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the first runtime contract from the screenshot security policy design is implemented without touching persistence cleanup, sensitive screenshot gates, or rolling screenshot retention.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/artifacts/2026-05-01-us035-rf017-screenshot-security-policy-design.md section 12 Story 1. Keep this story scoped to payload contract and live MIME-aware rendering.

userStories.title: Screenshot Persistence Sanitization
userStories.description: Implement the next screenshot security-policy slice by stripping screenshot base64 from new durable workflow result and wrap-up JSON writes while preserving path plus metadata for durable evidence records.
acceptance: New workflow_runs.result_json writes omit stepResults[].screenshotBase64
New wrap-up artifact JSON writes omit screenshots[].base64
Sanitized screenshot records retain path, stepId, source, class, mimeType, sensitivity, retention, and persistBase64 false when available
Runtime WorkflowResult objects returned before persistence remain compatible and are not stripped prematurely
session_screenshots remains path-only unless an existing durable query path requires a minimal metadata extension
Historical workflow_runs.result_json and wrap-up artifacts containing screenshot base64 remain documented legacy data with no bulk migration
Sensitive-phase gates, GET /api/screenshot binding, MCP audit, rolling cleanup, encryption, manual-review deletion logs, and fail-closed authoring validation remain out of scope
Focused tests prove durable persistence omits screenshot base64 while preserving metadata
jq empty prd.json passes
pnpm run typecheck passes
Focused persistence and wrap-up tests pass
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when new durable workflow persistence cannot embed screenshot base64 in SQLite result JSON or wrap-up artifact JSON, path plus metadata remains available for durable evidence, runtime result compatibility is preserved, and focused validation passes.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means new durable screenshot persistence is forward-sanitized: base64 bytes stay out of durable JSON, evidence screenshots are represented by path plus metadata, and runtime screenshot payload behavior from US-036 remains compatible.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/artifacts/2026-05-01-us035-rf017-screenshot-security-policy-design.md Story 2 and docs/debriefs/2026-05-01-screenshot-workflow-investigation-scratch-pad.md Story C. Keep this sanitize-first slice separate from sensitive gates, access gates, MCP audit, rolling cleanup, encryption, and historical migration.

userStories.title: Screenshot Capture Policy Gate
userStories.description: Implement the next screenshot security-policy slice by gating screenshot capture before pixels leave the browser, with allow/redact/step-scoped/block/evidence decisions, active UI/MCP binding, and step-scoped deletion.
acceptance: Screenshot capture requests flow through a shared policy decision helper or equivalent centralized contract
Policy can return allow, redact, step_scoped, evidence, or sensitive_blocked decisions
pii_wait capture attempts return structured denial or safe redaction behavior with no unredacted pixels
Sensitive-target step capture masks or redacts known sensitive regions before capture or fails closed
Step-scoped screenshots are deleted when the workflow advances to the next step
GET /api/screenshot rejects stale, mismatched, or unbound clients using the US-024 binding pattern
MCP screenshot capture is denied or structured-gated through the same policy decision
Rolling and live screenshots are paused, denied, or step-scoped during sensitive phases
Blocked screenshot responses include blockedReason and nextAction but no screenshot bytes
Telemetry records allow, redact, block, and step-TTL deletion decisions without image bytes
Existing US-036 payload and US-037 persistence behavior remain compatible
Historical migration, durable evidence encryption, signed deletion logs, broad session_screenshots normalization, and agentic/orchestrator output guard rail remain out of scope
jq empty prd.json passes
pnpm run typecheck passes
Focused UI/session/workflow/MCP tests pass for allow, redact, block, and step-TTL deletion paths
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when screenshot capture decisions are enforced before pixels leave the browser, sensitive phases cannot leak unredacted screenshots, temporary screenshots expire on step advance, UI/MCP access is bound to active context, and focused validation passes.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means screenshot capture is governed by an explicit runtime policy gate: sensitive pixels are redacted or denied, temporary screenshots expire on step advance, approved evidence remains path-plus-metadata only, and agents receive structured next-action metadata instead of blind failures.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from US-035 policy, US-036 payload contract, US-037 persistence sanitization, US-024 binding pattern, and the screenshot workflow scratch-pad decision to broaden Story D from Sensitive Screenshot Gate to Screenshot Capture Policy Gate. Keep agentic/orchestrator output handling as a guard rail unless mode: agentic remains in production screenshot use.

userStories.title: Screenshot Retention Cleanup, Scavenger, And Evidence Audit
userStories.description: Implement screenshot retention cleanup and evidence audit so rolling/debug files are removed according to policy, orphaned files are reconciled, cleanup failures are retryable, and evidence screenshots have stable who/what/when/why audit trails.
acceptance: Rolling/debug screenshots are deleted on successful wrap-up unless debug retention is enabled
Failed/debug rolling artifacts are retained only until ttl_24h cleanup eligibility
Startup scavenger processes bounded work units and does not block browser/session availability
Wrap-up scavenger reconciles current-session files
Cleanup failures create durable retry or dead-letter records
Evidence screenshots receive stable evidence ids and capture-time content hashes
Evidence audit records who, what, when, why, action state, target path, evidence id, and content hash without screenshot bytes
Evidence deletion records pending_deletion before unlink, deleted only after verified unlink, and delete_failed on failure
TTL precedence uses session end time, then capture timestamp, then filesystem mtime fallback
WebSocket invalidation is broadcast after verified evidence deletion
Cleanup telemetry is aggregated by batch and contains no screenshot bytes
Step-scoped screenshots remain transient and are not eligible for evidence audit or manual review

Screenshot capture gates, redaction, durable base64 sanitization, payload contract changes, encryption-at-rest, broad historical migration, and agentic/orchestrator output guard rail remain out of scope
jq empty prd.json passes
pnpm run typecheck passes
Focused cleanup/audit/scavenger tests pass
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when rolling/debug screenshot retention cleanup is enforced, orphaned files can be reconciled, cleanup failures are durable and retryable, evidence screenshots have stable identity plus capture-time hash, manual evidence deletion is audit-safe, and focused validation passes.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means non-evidence screenshot files are cleaned up according to retention policy, cleanup failures are durable and retryable, and retained evidence screenshots have stable identity plus verified who/what/when/why audit trails.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/debriefs/2026-05-02-screenshot-retention-cleanup-evidence-audit-scratch-pad.md and the edge-case reconciliation findings. Keep this story scoped to retention cleanup, scavenger, failed-deletion retry, and evidence audit. Do not reopen US-036, US-037, or US-038 behavior.

userStories.title: Deterministic Reddit Duplicate Evidence Producer
userStories.description: Replace the direct Reddit duplicate-check browser-use producer with deterministic TypeScript/Playwright title collection and bounded Jaccard scoring while preserving the existing evidence contract and submit gate.
acceptance: Direct post_to_reddit no longer uses browser-use for check_duplicate_reddit_post evidence production
The deterministic output passes the existing Reddit duplicate evidence parser
EXTRACTED_TITLES contains the bounded collected titles
OVERLAP_SCORES contains title and score objects for collected titles
Any score >= 0.70 yields DUPLICATE_CHECK_RESULT: DUPLICATE_RISK and MATCHING_TITLE
Scores >= 0.50 and < 0.70 remain visible but do not create a third canonical result
Scores < 0.50 yield DUPLICATE_CHECK_RESULT: NO_DUPLICATE_FOUND
Zero usable titles, selector drift, or navigation failure fails closed and blocks submit
Existing submit_reddit_post missing-evidence and duplicate-risk gates remain intact
Screenshot capture scheduler and post-task TTL cleanup remain out of scope
jq empty prd.json passes
pnpm run typecheck passes
Focused workflow tests pass
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when direct post_to_reddit produces deterministic duplicate-check evidence without browser-use for check_duplicate_reddit_post, the existing parser and submit gate still enforce duplicate safety, DOM drift fails closed, and focused validation passes.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the direct Reddit workflow can deterministically collect recent subreddit titles, compute bounded Jaccard duplicate evidence, and feed the existing submit gate without relying on browser-use for the duplicate-check evidence producer.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/debriefs/2026-05-03-reddit-duplicate-check-stall-blast-radius.md and docs/debriefs/2026-05-03-reddit-screenshot-recovery-implementation-story-plan.md. Keep this story scoped to deterministic Reddit duplicate evidence production; do not implement the screenshot capture scheduler, post-task TTL cleanup, or agentic retirement here.

userStories.title: Screenshot Capture Scheduler And Hung-Step Guardrail
userStories.description: Coordinate screenshot capture requests through a scheduler so UI live frames, MCP requests, workflow evidence screenshots, and rolling debug frames do not create avoidable capture contention or unbounded debug growth during stalled workflow steps.
acceptance: Concurrent UI live-frame requests do not launch duplicate page.screenshot calls
Collapsed UI requests preserve stable UI behavior
Workflow evidence screenshot requests are not starved by UI or rolling captures
MCP screenshot requests use the same scheduler and existing screenshot policy gate
Rolling debug capture is paused or throttled after the same step exceeds the hung threshold
Step advance resets the hung-step rolling guardrail
Sensitive-phase blocking and redaction behavior from US-038 remains compatible
Screenshot telemetry includes top-level stepId where the current workflow step is known
Scheduler telemetry is byte-free and records queued, collapsed, executed, throttled, and skipped decisions
Post-task 120s screenshot TTL cleanup remains out of scope
jq empty prd.json passes
pnpm run typecheck passes
Focused session/UI/MCP/workflow tests pass
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when screenshot capture requests are coordinated through a scheduler, duplicate live/debug captures collapse or yield to higher-priority evidence, rolling debug capture is paused or throttled during hung steps, telemetry reflects scheduler decisions without screenshot bytes, and focused validation passes.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means screenshot capture is coordinated so redundant live/debug captures no longer create avoidable contention, evidence screenshots are prioritized, rolling debug capture cannot grow unchecked during hung steps, and existing screenshot policy behavior remains intact.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/debriefs/2026-05-03-reddit-duplicate-check-stall-blast-radius.md and docs/debriefs/2026-05-03-reddit-screenshot-recovery-implementation-story-plan.md. Keep this story scoped to capture scheduling and hung-step rolling guardrails; do not implement US-042 post-task TTL cleanup here.

userStories.title: Post-Task Screenshot TTL Cleanup And Recovery
userStories.description: Add a success-only 120 second post-task cleanup window for rolling/debug screenshots with startup recovery for lost timers, while preserving failed-run debug retention and evidence/manual-review screenshots.
acceptance: Successful workflow runs schedule a 120000ms post-task cleanup for rolling/debug screenshots
Successful-run rolling/debug files remain available before TTL expiry
Successful-run rolling/debug files are deleted after TTL expiry
Failed and aborted runs do not use the 120s success cleanup path and remain under ttl_24h
Startup recovery deletes successful-run rolling/debug artifacts whose timer was lost and whose TTL expired
Rolling directory cleanup is explicitly covered and does not rely only on WorkflowResult.screenshots
Evidence and keep_until_manual_review screenshots are preserved
Failed unlink operations retry with backoff and record screenshot_cleanup_failures when still failing
Cleanup telemetry is byte-free and records scheduled, completed, and failed outcomes
US-036 through US-043 screenshot/workflow contracts remain compatible
jq empty prd.json passes
pnpm run typecheck passes
Focused retention/workflow tests pass
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when successful-run rolling/debug files have a bounded 120 second post-task lifetime, failed/aborted debug frames keep the existing ttl_24h behavior, restart recovery prevents permanent successful-run rolling orphans, evidence is preserved, and focused validation passes.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means successful-run rolling/debug screenshots have a bounded 120 second post-task lifetime with startup recovery for lost timers, while failed/aborted run debug frames and evidence/manual-review screenshots keep their existing retention protections.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from docs/debriefs/2026-05-03-reddit-duplicate-check-stall-blast-radius.md and docs/debriefs/2026-05-03-reddit-screenshot-recovery-implementation-story-plan.md. Keep this story scoped to post-task screenshot TTL cleanup and recovery; do not alter Reddit submit gates, US-043 LLM evidence, or screenshot payload/security contracts.

userStories.title: LLM Post-Action Review Evidence Contract
userStories.description: Capture LLM/browser-use post-action reasoning as structured evidence that deterministic workflow gates can corroborate, reject, or escalate to HITL review after irreversible browser side effects.
acceptance: Side-effecting agent_task results can carry structured post-action review evidence
Reddit submit accepts current /comments/<id> URLs as success
Reddit submit accepts ?created=t3_<id> redirects only when matching title/body or canonical URL evidence corroborates creation
Malformed or missing LLM review evidence cannot bypass deterministic gates
LLM-success/deterministic-failure disagreement enters HITL review instead of a blind terminal failure
HITL review state includes step id, deterministic failure reason, LLM success signal, current URL, canonical URL, and match status where available
Telemetry is byte-free and records post-action evidence parsed, accepted, rejected, or escalated
Existing duplicate-check, approval, and postcondition tests continue to pass
US-042 post-task screenshot TTL cleanup remains out of scope
jq empty prd.json passes
pnpm run typecheck passes
Focused workflow/UI tests pass
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when the workflow kernel can use structured LLM post-action review as corroborating evidence for Reddit submit outcomes, false failures caused by Reddit created-post redirects are eliminated, and disagreements route to HITL review with enough context for an operator to decide.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means LLM/browser-use post-action reasoning is captured as typed, auditable evidence that deterministic postcondition gates can corroborate, reject, or escalate to HITL without treating the LLM as unchecked authority.
userStories.technicalNotes: Created after the 2026-05-03 live r/test status-update run posted successfully but failed the workflow because Reddit redirected to /r/test/?created=t3_1t2zy4d instead of leaving the browser on /comments/. Keep US-042 reserved for post-task screenshot TTL cleanup.

userStories.title: Served Terminal State Reset And Graceful Closeout Handler
userStories.description: Add a complete terminal-state reset and graceful closeout handler for the long-running ai-vision served process so failed or completed workflow states can be acknowledged and cleared to idle without restarting the app.
acceptance: Served terminal complete and error states can be acknowledged and reset to idle without restarting the process
Terminal reset works when the browser has already been closed
Terminal reset does not call hitlCoordinator.returnControl when no active wait exists
Active non-terminal HITL waits still require the existing HITL control APIs
/api/status returns idle after terminal reset
Connected UI clients receive an idle websocket state after terminal reset
Screenshot polling after reset does not keep rendering stale workflow context
Mismatched session reset attempts are rejected
Active non-terminal reset attempts are rejected
Served SIGINT and SIGTERM close servers and browser/session resources best-effort
Telemetry is byte-free and records reset requested, completed, rejected, and shutdown events
Existing wrap-up, US-042, and US-043 tests remain compatible
jq empty prd.json passes
pnpm run typecheck passes
Focused workflow/UI tests pass
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when the served ai-vision UI can recover from a terminal failed or completed workflow without process restart, and the next workflow can start from clean idle state while durable workflow history remains intact.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means a terminal served workflow can be reviewed, acknowledged, cleared to idle, and followed by another workflow without restarting ai-vision serve, while active HITL waits and durable workflow history remain protected.
userStories.technicalNotes: Created after the 2026-05-03 served app retained terminal phase=error for submit_reddit_post while the browser was already closed and /api/screenshot returned Browser not started. Keep this story scoped to served terminal reset and shutdown handling; do not alter Reddit postconditions, US-042 screenshot TTL cleanup, or US-043 LLM evidence.
